name: SmartSolar MPPT 75/15
notes: Entry-level MPPT for small 12V/24V systems. Suitable for caravans, small cabins, and auxiliary battery charging. Max PV power ~220W at 12V, ~440W at 24V.

name: SmartSolar MPPT 100/20
notes: Compact 12V/24V MPPT. The 100V input limit keeps string voltages low — maximum of 2 standard residential panels in series. Common for off-grid cabins and boats.

name: SmartSolar MPPT 150/45
notes: Mid-range SmartSolar. Bridges the gap between small 12/24V systems and the larger 48V RS range. Suitable for up to 3 standard panels in series on a 48V bus.

name: SmartSolar MPPT 150/70
notes: High-current 150V unit. Supports 48V systems but the 150V input cap limits you to 3 panels in series maximum — below the threshold where most modern high-Voc panels become usable.

name: SmartSolar MPPT 250/100
notes: Workhorse for medium 24V and 48V systems. The 250V input cap allows 4-5 standard residential panels in series without Voc issues. Popular for ground-mount arrays and small commercial roofs.

name: SmartSolar MPPT 250/100 MC4
notes: Identical to the 250/100 but with built-in MC4 connectors. Removes the need for a separate MC4-to-terminal adapter. Preferred for installations where the charger is installed close to the panels in a weatherproof enclosure.

name: SmartSolar RS 450/100 (Primary)
notes: High-voltage residential MPPT. The 450V input allows long strings of 6–9 panels in series. The RS 450/100 unit contains two independent 18A trackers — one primary input and one secondary. The high 120V startup threshold is critical: short arrays of 3 or fewer low-Voc panels may fail to start on hot summer days.

name: SmartSolar RS 450/100 (Tracker 2)
notes: The second independent MPPT tracker on a single RS 450/100 unit. Assign a second string to this entry to model both trackers of one physical device. Price is £0 — this is not a separate hardware purchase.

name: SmartSolar RS 450/200 (Primary)
notes: The RS 450/200 contains four independent 18A MPPT trackers in a single enclosure. The 200A output enables significantly higher PV power on a single 48V system. Each tracker is independently limited to 18A input so string current must be carefully managed.

name: SmartSolar RS 450/200 (Trackers 2–4)
notes: One of the three additional MPPT trackers in a single RS 450/200 unit. Assign additional strings here to model all four trackers of one physical device. Price is £0 — not a separate hardware purchase.

name: Solis S6 Hybrid 6kW
notes: Popular hybrid inverter with dual built-in MPPT. The 600V PV input accepts longer strings than most Victron RS units. Good value for systems that don't require the deep Victron ecosystem integration. Cloud monitoring and grid-tied export included.

name: Solis S6 Hybrid 10kW
notes: 10kW variant of the Solis S6 hybrid. Higher AC output but same dual-MPPT PV limits. Note: the built-in MPPT architecture is less flexible than a Victron MPPT + MultiPlus DC-coupled system for battery management.

name: Growatt SPH 6000
notes: Budget-friendly dual-MPPT hybrid. Well-regarded in cost-sensitive retrofits. Good cloud monitoring included. Less customisable integration compared to Victron, but the PV-side electrical limits are competitive.

name: Growatt SPH 10000
notes: 10kW Growatt hybrid. Same dual-MPPT limits as the 6kW variant. Strong value for whole-home systems. Verify maximum string Voc carefully against panel temperature coefficients — the 600V limit leaves less headroom than the Victron RS 450.

name: SolarEdge Home Hub 6kW
notes: WARNING: Requires SolarEdge power optimisers on every panel — a mandatory per-panel hardware cost not included in this price. The optimiser architecture eliminates string voltage mismatch losses and enables panel-level monitoring, but removes string voltage flexibility. The effective PV input voltage to the inverter is fixed by the optimiser topology, not the string length.

name: GivEnergy Gen 3 Hybrid 3.6kW
notes: Market-leading UK brand. Highly integrated ecosystem with excellent app. 3.6kW export limit friendly to UK DNO G98 regulations.

name: GivEnergy Gen 3 Hybrid 5.0kW
notes: 5.0kW variant of the popular Gen 3. Requires G99 DNO approval in the UK. Best paired with the GivEnergy 9.5kWh battery.

name: Sunsynk 3.6kW Hybrid Parity
notes: Extremely popular in the UK. Unrivaled for off-grid operations, EPS (Emergency Power Supply), and generator integration. G98 compliant.

name: Sunsynk 5kW Hybrid Parity
notes: The 5kW powerhouse from Sunsynk. Very flexible configuration. Allows unbalanced tracking and huge DC oversizing.

name: Sunsynk 8kW Hybrid Parity
notes: Massive 8kW single-phase output for heavy UK domestic loads (heat pumps, EVs). Requires G99 DNO approval.

name: Fox ESS H1 3.7kW Hybrid
notes: High voltage battery architecture. Widely installed by UK social housing and new-build developers due to sharp pricing and reliability.

name: Fox ESS H1 5.0kW Hybrid
notes: Popular 5kW hybrid from Fox ESS. Uses high voltage modular battery stacks (Mira/ECS) for greater efficiency than 48V systems.

name: LuxPower LXP 3600 Hybrid
notes: Cost-effective 3.6kW hybrid inverter. Widely paired with Pylontech or Greenleaf 48V batteries in budget-friendly UK installs.

name: LuxPower Squirrel Pod 5.0kW
notes: Squirrel Pod (LXP 5000) hybrid. Offers flexible parallel networking and good integration with UK agile tariffs.

name: Sofar HYD 3600-EP
notes: Dependable G98-compliant 3.6kW inverter. Great EPS changeover speed and passive cooling (no noisy fans).

name: Sofar HYD 5000-EP
notes: 5.0kW variant. Broadly compatible with almost all 48V rack-mounted batteries (Pylontech, AMAS, Greenur).
